title: cistus × incanus subsp. villosus
intro: Cistus × incanus subsp. villosus, also known as Rock Rose or Hairless Rock Rose, is a hybrid plant species within the Cistus genus, characterized by its hairy stems, small white flowers, and a woody, shrub-like appearance. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and infections. The health benefits of Rock Rose include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a valuable addition to herbal remedies for skin and respiratory issues. Rock Rose can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, salves, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
